findings.description: Ensure table headers have discernible text
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers [<th scope="col"></th>]; Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers [<th scope="col"></th>]; Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers [<th scope="col"></th>] (and 10 more)

findings.description: Figure 1 (Total Number of Survey Sites - By Year bar graph) is missing as an image. The chart's axis labels, data values, and year labels are rendered as plain text instead of preserving the bar graph image.
findings.evidence: PDF page 9 shows a bar graph with bars for each year 2012-2023 with labeled values. HTML renders this as inline text: '7000 6564 6178 6503 6041 5954 6311 6000 5775 5714 5378...' with no chart image.

findings.description: Figure 2 (Lake Gaston Aquatic Plant Community - 2023 bar graph) is missing as an image. The chart's species names, categories, and percentage values are rendered as plain text instead of the grouped bar chart image.
findings.evidence: PDF page 9 shows a color-coded bar graph with species on x-axis and % Total Vegetation on y-axis. HTML renders this as inline text: '45 Emergent Submergent Floating Algae 40 35 30 25 20 15 10 5 0 American Lotus Arrowhead...'

findings.description: Figure 7 (Lake Gaston Hydrilla dual-axis chart showing estimated standing acreage bars and percent occurrence line) is missing as an image. Chart data is rendered as plain text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 14 shows a dual-axis bar/line chart with green bars (Acres) and dashed line (Rake Tosses) from 2012-2023. HTML renders this as text: '1800 70 Acres Rake Tosses 1600 60 1400 50 1200 40 1000 800 30 600 20 400 10 200 0 0 2012 2013...'

findings.description: Figure 9 (Lake Gaston Lyngbya dual-axis chart showing estimated standing acreage bars and percent occurrence line) is missing as an image. Chart data is rendered as plain text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 16 shows a dual-axis bar/line chart with pink bars (Acres) and dashed line from 2012-2023. HTML renders this as text: '1600 25 Acres 1400 20 1200 1000 15 800 10 600 400 5 200 0 0 2012 2013...'

findings.description: Figure 11 (Lake Gaston Eel Grass dual-axis chart showing estimated standing acreage bars and percent survey sites line) is missing as an image. Chart data is rendered as plain text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 18 shows a dual-axis bar/line chart with green bars (Acres) and dashed line (% Sites) from 2019-2023. HTML renders this as text: '5.0 140 Acres % Sites 4.5 120 4.0 3.5 100 3.0 80 2.5 60 2.0 1.5 40 1.0 20 0.5 0 0.0 2019 2020...'

findings.description: Chart data values from Figures 1, 2, 7, 9, and 11 are rendered as raw inline text sequences, creating nonsensical reading flow where a reader encounters strings of numbers and labels without visual chart context.
findings.evidence: For example, where Figure 7 should appear, the HTML presents '1800 70 Acres Rake Tosses 1600 60 1400 50 1200 40 1000 800 30 600 20 400 10 200 0 0 2012 2013 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 Acres Percent of Rake Tosses' as flowing text between the preceding paragraph and figure caption.

findings.description: Table 1 data is duplicated: it appears both as an embedded image (alt='Background shading and borders for Table 1') and as a fully-rendered HTML <table>. Same applies to Table 2.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains '' immediately followed by '
Table 1...' with all the same data. Same pattern repeats for Table 2.

findings.description: Both Tables 1 and 2 have awkward double-header structures where the table title ('LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023') is placed in the first cell of a <th> row with empty <th> siblings, then the actual column headers are in a second <tr>. In the PDF the title is a spanning header across the full width of the table.
findings.evidence: HTML Table 1 thead: first <tr> has '<th>LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023</th><th></th><th></th><th></th><th></th>', then second <tr> has the real column headers. PDF shows this as a single merged spanning title row.

findings.description: Alt text for table background images is inaccurate and non-descriptive — 'Background shading and borders for Table 1' and 'Background shading and borders for Table 2' describe decorative formatting rather than the substantive table content these images actually depict.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='Background shading and borders for Table 1' and alt='Background shading and borders for Table 2'. These images render the full visual tables from the PDF.
